description:
Rework cross-module source publication and related lookup seams to consume `ModuleInterface` instead of `module_exports`. `import only:` and `require only:` should use exact interface lookup, mint callable references lazily, and record requester provenance as obligations. Bare `import`/`require` and `except:` variants should use `exported_functions` / `exported_macros` and wait on `ModuleInterface` when the full set is not yet available.

Remote source-tier callable lookup should stop reading exports directly or treating `ModuleDefined` as a visibility gate.

Signal: `apply_import`, `apply_require`, and source-tier remote callable lookup become thin orchestration over interface queries, and compiler2 stops duplicating export-selection policy in source publication.
title: Compiler2 interface consumers: move import/require and cross-module source lookup onto interface queries
